<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Add missing CSI sequences" completed</summary>
<result>Both `zig build` and `zig build test` complete cleanly with no errors or warnings.

## Changes Made

- `/Users/zchee/src/github.com/zchee/agentmux/src/screen/writer.zig:200-207`: Added `eraseChars` method that blanks `n` cells at the cursor position in-place without shifting.

- `/Users/zchee/src/github.com/zchee/agentmux/src/input_handler.zig:145-169`: Added five new CSI handlers before the existing `'n'` case: `'X'` (ECH - erase characters), `'b'` (REP - repeat last character), `'s'` (ANSI cursor save), `'u'` (ANSI cursor restore), `'g'` (TBC - tab clear, no-op).

- `/Users/zchee/src/github.com/zchee/agentmux/src/input_handler.zig:406-421`: Added `test "process ECH erase characters"` verifying that `\x1b[4X` erases 4 cells at col 3 (0-based) while leaving surrounding characters intact.

## Verification

- Build: `zig build` -> pass (no output)
- Tests: `zig build test` -> pass (no output, Zig silent on all-pass)

## Summary

Implemented ECH (`CSI X`), REP (`CSI b`), ANSI cursor save/restore (`CSI s`/`CSI u`), and TBC (`CSI g`) escape sequence handlers, backed by the new `eraseChars` writer method, with a focused test confirming ECH behavior.</result>

task:
<task-notification>
<task-id>a074f54d56c43e96d</task-id>
<tool-use-id>REDACTED</tool-use-id>
<output-file>/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-zchee-src-github-com-zchee-agentmux/715a3c97-eb4e-41f0-85c6-aba05ad3f169/tasks/a074f54d56c43e96d.output</output-file>
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Implement format conditionals" completed</summary>
<result>All tests pass. Here is the summary.

## Changes Made

- `/Users/zchee/src/github.com/zchee/agentmux/src/status/format.zig:69-122`: Replaced the flat `}` scan in the `'{'` branch with a depth-tracking scan that extracts the full `content` between `#{` and its matching `}`. Then dispatches on the content prefix:
  - `?` — conditional: splits on the first two top-level commas via `findComma`, resolves the variable with `resolveVariable`, recursively expands the chosen branch with `expand`
  - `==:` — equality: splits on the first top-level comma, recursively expands both sides, emits `'1'` or `'0'`
  - `l:` — string length: recursively expands the argument, emits its `.len` as decimal
  - fallback — plain variable via the existing `appendVariable` helper

- `/Users/zchee/src/github.com/zchee/agentmux/src/status/format.zig:163-182`: Added `findComma` with proper nested `#{}` depth tracking so that `#{==:#{session_name},main}` finds the right delimiter.

- `/Users/zchee/src/github.com/zchee/agentmux/src/status/format.zig:195-271`: Added 5 new tests: `expand conditional true`, `expand conditional false`, `expand comparison equal`, `expand comparison not equal`, `expand string length`.

## Verification

- Build/Tests: `zig build test` → exit 0 (all tests pass, no output = no failures in Zig's test runner)

## Summary

Implemented tmux-compatible `#{?var,true,false}` conditionals, `#{==:a,b}` equality comparison, and `#{l:str}` string length into the format engine. All three forms support nested `#{}` expressions on their arguments via recursive `expand` calls, and `findComma` was made depth-aware to correctly handle those
